message:
perf(vdbe): promote SCopy into try_execute_hot_opcode hot-path

Third opcode-promotion lever on bd-1dp9.6.2, following AddImm (64d8ce4d)
and Copy (20b05a95). `Opcode::SCopy` is the shallow single-register copy
emitted by codegen when a value needs to be moved between registers
without `Copy`'s range semantics. The body is `clone + set_reg_fast` —
the same simple shape as single-register `Copy` — so the dispatch
shortening transfers directly.

Mirrors the existing main-match arm verbatim (un-materialized
`get_reg(..).clone()`). The pre-existing arm does not materialize
`make_record_lookaside` sideband state before cloning, and the hot-path
arm intentionally preserves that exact behaviour to avoid drift — if
codegen ever does emit `SCopy(armed-target, ..)`, both arms need a
consistent fix in a separate commit (see 2ba72570 for the pattern used
to fix the same class of gap in `Copy`).

Also adds a parallel `vdbe_pipeline_execute_scopy` microbench and a
regression test `test_scopy_register_via_hot_path` pinning the hot-path
arm's clone + write semantics.

Before/after on the new bench (criterion, p<0.05):

  vdbe_pipeline_execute_scopy/256   3.4872 us   ->  2.5035 us  (+38.6% thrpt, p=0.00)
  vdbe_pipeline_execute_scopy/1024  13.879 us   ->  9.9629 us  (+37.8% thrpt, p=0.00)

AddImm + Copy bench re-runs show no regression from this change: AddImm
/256 2.8643 us / /1024 11.260 us unchanged vs post-AddImm baseline; Copy
/256 3.00 us / /1024 11.9 us drift is attributable to the sideband fix
in 2ba72570 (adds `clone_reg_materialized` overhead to every Copy), not
to SCopy promotion.

Bench: `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_cc2_vdbe_perf \
  cargo bench -p fsqlite-vdbe --bench pipeline_stages -- \
  vdbe_pipeline_execute_scopy --warm-up-time 1 --measurement-time 4`

`test_scopy_register_via_hot_path` + existing `test_copy_register`,
`test_copy_materializes_make_record_sideband_before_copying`,
`test_intcopy_coerces`, and `test_add_imm*` all pass. Clippy (lib +
tests + benches) clean via rch.

Bead: bd-1dp9.6.2

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
